description:
India Book III, 55a: Letter from Perahya b. Yosef Yiju to his brother Shemuel in Fustat, complaining of his failure to procure a responsum (see III, 55). Perahya also includes brief remarks on their mother’s illness and his apology for being unable to visit, a request for Shemuel’s assistance in collecting a debt and in paying the capitation tax, and a one-line inquiry about whether his brother has made a bible amulet or ankle for his son, to protect him. Probably sent from Mahalla, late twelfth century. (S. D. Goitein and Mordechai Friedman, India Book, 790-1)

Friedman-Goitein translated only lines 12-16.
tags: Illness, IB3, ib-partial translation-eng, India, illness letter 969-1517
description_he: ספר הודו III,55א: מכתב מפרחיה בן יוסף יג'ו לאחיו שמואל, על מחלת האם, גבייה ובקשה לקמיע לבנו
